danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile entities in level spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates threat to player; collision results in damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage-specific obstacles and traps based on historical era themes
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger; player must navigate or avoid
Player proximity to game-over state; multiple hits reduce remaining lives

safety_signals.meaning: Safe to approach; rewards exploration and completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Special items including Nerf Ballzooka gun
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety boost; grants offensive capability against threats
Player agency; safe decision-making space between action sequences

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bubsy character sprite and sidekick Arnold
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Playable character presence; rescue mission objective indicators

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile entities spawned within the Amazatorium theme park
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Amazatorium virtual worlds; historical era-themed spaces representing stolen environments
Rescue targets within park; multiplayer mode participants

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Virtual world environments based on historical eras; stage variety reflecting different time periods and aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies and obstacles themed to match era-specific environments; level design varies by historical setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Theme park interior accessible; security systems or night-time aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heightened enemy presence; urgency to rescue kidnapped relatives before opening day

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Time machine theft in progress (background conflict)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Historical artifact theft theme visually embedded in stage designs; stolen items visible as collectible targets or obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Narrative urgency drives rescue mission; player must navigate through contaminated historical environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three-hit damage system; visual health indicator feedback on HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased vulnerability; loss of protection forces defensive platforming strategy

environmental_storytelling.example: Each stage represents distinct historical period through visual design (virtual worlds within Amazatorium); narrative context that Oinker stole from history and recreated them
Terri and Terry kidnapped; Bubsy and Arnold pursue; visual presence of younger relatives as goal indicators within park levels
Amazatorium itself is Oinker's constructed world; all visual design reflects his corporate/entrepreneurial corruption; park serves as manifestation of antagonist's ambition
Non-linear stage selection (improvement over first game's linearity) visually communicates expanded world; player can explore multiple historical eras in chosen order

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Jumping, gliding, collecting marbles; standard platformer movement and interaction
Can shoot Nerf Ballzooka gun; offensive capability against enemies and obstacles
Takes up to three hits before losing a life; visual damage feedback; reduced protection
Patrol and attack; themed to historical era environments; collision damage to player
Support presence; narrative companion in rescue mission
Controlled by second player; alternate control scheme; assists in dual-character progression

notes: Bubsy II uses visual world-state communication primarily through stage design variety and environmental theming rather than dynamic visual shifts. The Amazatorium's virtual historical worlds serve as both gameplay environments and narrative manifestation of Oinker's crimes. The non-linear stage selection system provides visual agency signals to the player. Limited faction differentiation beyond protagonist/antagonist binary; faction identity relies on character presence and park ownership rather than color coding or heraldic symbols. Danger signals are conveyed through classic platformer design (enemies, hazards, health system) rather than systemic visual warnings. Historical era theming communicates both world-state and environmental storytelling simultaneously.
